title: Cooking Oil Smoke Points & Nutrition Comparison 2026
description: The definitive comparison of every cooking oil — smoke point, fat composition, calories, best uses.

schemas.headline: Cooking Oil Smoke Points & Nutrition Comparison 2026
schemas.description: The definitive comparison of every cooking oil — smoke point, fat composition, calories, best uses.

mainEntity.name: What oil has the highest smoke point?

acceptedAnswer.text: Avocado oil has one of the highest smoke points at 520°F (271°C), making it excellent for deep frying and high-heat cooking.

mainEntity.name: Is avocado oil better than olive oil for cooking?

acceptedAnswer.text: For high-heat cooking, avocado oil is better due to its 520°F smoke point compared to olive oil's 350°F-465°F. For lower heat or finishing, olive oil is excellent.

mainEntity.name: What is the healthiest cooking oil?

acceptedAnswer.text: Extra virgin olive oil and avocado oil are widely considered the healthiest options due to their high monounsaturated fat content and beneficial plant compounds.

mainEntity.name: Can you fry with olive oil?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you can pan-fry with light or refined olive oil (smoke point 465°F). Extra virgin olive oil (smoke point 350°F) is better for low-heat cooking or finishing.

mainEntity.name: What oil should I use for deep frying?

acceptedAnswer.text: Oils with high smoke points and neutral flavors are best for deep frying, such as avocado oil, peanut oil, safflower oil, and canola oil.
